texto: It was even said that some birds spring to life in the tension of accidental chords being struck in the ether, a confluence of arbitrary sound waves from unrelated sources: a piano, a truck, a breaking bottle---a bird.
explicacao: Citação de Carl Watson sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “It was even said that some birds spring to life in the tension of accidental chords being struck …” articula reflexao, birth, nature em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como nature e spontaneous-generation.

texto: There was one floor that was all gynecologists. They could tell by the remnants of weird optical contraptions- all the convoluted tools men use when they're searching for the source of their anxieties.
explicacao: Citação de Carl Watson sobre temas ligados a humor. “There was one floor that was all gynecologists. They could tell by the remnants of weird optical …” articula humor, irony, there em registro de humor, com relevância para contextos como irony.

frase_original: There was one floor that was all gynecologists. They could tell by the remnants of weird optical contraptions- all the convoluted tools men use when they're searching for the source of their anxieties.

texto: I saw a bullfrog snap a tiny bird off a reed once, swallowing it just like that, grinding the singing thing to foodstuff in its slimy belly, and the witnessing of that act produced in me an insight: that this moment of terror before resignation was possibly the only ascertainable moment of truth we can ever know in life.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Carl Watson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “I saw a bullfrog snap a tiny bird off a reed once, swallowing it just like that, grinding the sin…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, vida, survival pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de survival e terror.

frase_original: I saw a bullfrog snap a tiny bird off a reed once, swallowing it just like that, grinding the singing thing to foodstuff in its slimy belly, and the witnessing of that act produced in me an insight: that this moment of terror before resignation was possibly the only ascertainable moment of truth we can ever know in life.
